publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-01-21 23:14:38 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Includes papers of the "Universal Friend," her followers, and their descendants, relating largely to the migration of Wilkinson and her followers from Rhode Island to what is now Yates County, New York, the development of their settlements, their conflict and litigation over land, and their religious life. Included are minutes of meetings, deeds, the last will of the "Universal Friend" and others, journals, sermons, accounts, documents covering the Boston and Massachusetts Pre-emptions, the Gore Land Case, the Yates County Bank, the Second National Bank, President Jackson, temperance, and other subjects; and letters to and from Jemima Wilkinson, Sarah Richards, Ruth Richards, Rachel Malin, the Malin family, Alice Hazzard, James Parker, Arnold Potter, Thomas Hathaway, William Carter, Ruth Spencer, William Turpin, and others.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Originally a Rhode Island Quaker, Jemima Wilkinson believed that she had died following an illness in 1776, and later arose as the "Publick Universal Friend," who was sent by God to preach against sin. After preaching in Rhode Island, Connecticut, and Pennsylvania, she established a colony (1790) on Keuka Lake in Western New York for herself and her followers. Soon after the Friend's death in 1819, the community disintegrated entirely.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Includes papers of the "Universal Friend," her followers, and their descendants, relating largely to the migration of Wilkinson and her followers from Rhode Island to what is now Yates County, New York, the development of their settlements, their conflict and litigation over land, and their religious life. Included are minutes of meetings, deeds, the last will of the "Universal Friend" and others, journals, sermons, accounts, documents covering the Boston and Massachusetts Pre-emptions, the Gore Land Case, the Yates County Bank, the Second National Bank, President Jackson, temperance, and other subjects; and letters to and from Jemima Wilkinson, Sarah Richards, Ruth Richards, Rachel Malin, the Malin family, Alice Hazzard, James Parker, Arnold Potter, Thomas Hathaway, William Carter, Ruth Spencer, William Turpin, and others.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Microfilm only, originals returned to donor.
More items of Jemina Wilkinison, including some originals as well as microfilm, are available in collection #621.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Jemima Wilkinson papers, #357. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

p.extref: A copy of the printed guide is available by clicking here. (.5 MB Searchable PDF)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Walley Adams, Dartmouth, Massachusetts Bay to James Hathaway, Dartmouth

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: To the Universal Friend. No signature, but initials M. T. Sometime in the third month, 1782.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Lydia Mevrick, Charlestown, South Carolina, to an unknown

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Unsigned, from Worcester, to "Dearest Friend". Arrived safely in Worcester - good road by way of New York - religious discussion - wishes the Friend would come there.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to the Public Universal Friend, at Pennsylvania, from James Parker, Jerusalem. Describes life in new settlement and projects.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Sarah Pritchard, Worcester, to Ruth Pritchard, South Kingston, Rhode Island State, New England. Mentions many friends, and "Friend" sends love to all.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from William Carter, Albany, to Richard Smith, Jerusalem. Discussion of a case which Enoch Malin is bringing, as a result of the defective will of Sarah Malin, involving certain lands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Latham Avery, Groton, to Silas Burrows. Concerning daughter's marriage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benedict Robinson, Jamestown, Rhode Island, and David Wagener, Worcester, Pennsylvania. Concerning division of land.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benedict Robinson to Sarah Richards, Worcester, Pennsylvania. Twenty Four silver dollars.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from William Carter, Albany, to the Universal Friend, Jerusalem. Since returning to Albany, and declaring himself a Friend, his friends have forsaken him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes in a case involving a bond given by William Potter and Thomas Hathaway to the community of Friends residing at Jerusalem, for 5000 pounds.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of meetings to elect trustees for Society of the Universal Friend

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Benedict Robinson, Jerusalem, will allow Sarah Richards to owe him money without interest until she can pay it.

did.unittitle: Rachel Malin vs. Enoch and Eliza Malin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In Chancery to the Honorable John Lansing Junior Esquire Chancellor of the State of New York Amended Bill in the case Rachel Malin vs. Enoch Malin and Eliza his wife, Elnathan Botsford, Asahel Stone, Asa Ingraham and Truman Stone

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipt to Mr. Benedict Robinson for one deed. Signed by Nath Gesham and Oliver Phelps "and one other signed by himself to Sara Richards to be recorded," Canandaigua.

did.unittitle: Rachel Malin vs. Enoch and Eliza Malin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In Chancery to the Honorable John Lansing Junior Esquire Chancellor of the State of New York. Amended Bill in the case Rachel Malin vs. Enoch Malin and Eliza his wife, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas Hathaway to Sarah Richards, Jerusalem, County of Ontario. A piece of land lying in Ontario County in a township No. 7 Range 2, Lot No. 47

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sarah Richards in 1793 to Ruth Pritchard as to the land from Thomas Hathaway bought for the Friend.

did.unittitle: Evidence given in Gore Land Case

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Remarks on evidence given in Gore Land Case arising from distribution by Parker, Potter, and Hathaway.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Hathaway of Jerusalem in Ontario County to Rachel Malin of Jerusalem, Ontario County. Farm implements and household articles.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: William Carter to Rachel Malin of Ontario County. A piece of land, lots 21 and 22 in Township 7, Range 2 in the Massachusetts preemption.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Sarah Potter of South Kingston to "Friend". All well - reminiscence over old times together.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Dr. Rachel Malin to William Carter and statement to William Carter of his part of the cost, time, and expenses in plowing and conveying his part of the land in Township 7.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thomas Hathaway to William Carter. All rights in lands except stated 5,960 acres.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Made at Jerusalem that he has recorded with Richard Smith and Rachel Malin claims against State of Sarah Richards.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: James Hathaway of Jerusalem, Ontario County to Rachel Malin of same town and county. Tract of land in Township 7 in second rage of Massachusetts preemption.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record of proceedings at meetings of Society of Universal friend by John Briggs, Clerk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of Court Record stating that Richard Smith exhibited his Bill as executor of will of Sarah Richards at court of Probate in Waterburg.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hannah Baldwin of Jerusalem, Ontario County to Rachel Malin acting in trust for Publick Universal Friend two tracts of land containing 14, 140 acres.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from William Huast to John Briggs, Geneva, N.Y. Notes he is detained from starting to Albany. William Carter attempting to have bill filed in Chancery in case court gave judgement against Lisson. Plans to be in Albany to converse with Mr. Van Vechten on the subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Benedict Robinson to Universal Friend. Requests settlement of lands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from William Huast to Rachel Malin, Chenango Point. Enoch has deed for land he lives on. Expects to be at Ontario Court first week in November

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipt for Publick Universal Friends, Jerusalem, lands in the second , 7th town given by Benedict Robinson and Thomas Hathaway . Annual meetings of the Society of Universal Friends for the purpose of choosing trustees, November 17, 1806-1816

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Deposition of witnesses in Court in the case of Rachel Malin vs. Enoch Malin and his wife, Eliza, and others in the village of Canandaigua.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Receipt received of the Publick Universal Friend and Rachel Malin for all debts and demands, $80.00 by Margaret Malin and John Briggs, Jerusalem Village.

did.unittitle: R. W. Stoddard to Mrs. Eliza S. Malin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from R. W. Stoddard, Geneva, to Mrs. Eliza S. Malin, concerning the case with Rachel Malin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Will - Last testament of the Publick Universal Friend. All lands, household furniture, cattle, sheep, swine, horses and wearing apparel to be given to Rachel and Margaret Malin, all of Jerusalem, county of Ontario.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Rachel Malin, Jerusalem to Dr. Gold. Williams has no idea of compromising.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Received from Universal Friend by Rachel Ingraham, for service done, $20.00

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Margaret Malin, Jerusalem to her sister Abigail Malin, Philadelphia.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from John Gill to Rachel Malin, New Jerusalem. Wants the names of witnesses to each of the nine points listed for the charges against Cornwell, Hathaway, Domian and Guernsey.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from G. W. Malin to Aunt. Concerning reason for being in Burdette - engaged to M. Alfred, a merchant in Burdette. Asks her Aunt to furnish M. Hartwell with leather sufficient to make her a pair of boots, she has leather from Penn Yan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Received of Rachel Malin and James Brown - $29.70, in full for payment due on a contract for the building a certain saw mill. By J. B. Waterman, Jerusalem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fire Insurance Policy, Western Fire Insurance Company, Policy of William M. Oliver, Village of Penn Yan, Yates County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Received of Rachel Malin by the hand of Rowland Moseley to our full satisfaction for the right on claim if any which we have against the personal Estate of our grandmother Sarah Richards late of the County of Ontario and State of New York. David Malin and Avery L. Malin.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Rowland Moseley, Thompson to James Brown, Jerusalem, Yates County. Concerning sale of his land in Brown's vicinity; he may appoint Brown as power of attorney or move in the vicinity near it. Also speaks of calling on a Mr. Wood in Geneva as requested by Brown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of the Charter of the Yates County Bank, An Act to incorporate the President Directors and Company of the Yates County Bank. Passed April 2, 1831

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Be Temperate" by E. T. Benedict, Jerusalem.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of money expended for clothing etc. for Rebecca Cristons

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Marilla Marks to Margaret Malin, Oberlin. Tells of death and character of Rachel, matters concerning settlement of her estate. Poor health and hard work of James. Both she and husband like Oberlin. People Of Oberlin hide seventeen run away slaves from authorities.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Financial Statement - Bill for groceries and dry goods sold by Watson Stark and Company to James Brown.

did.unittitle: Lucy P. Morton to Dear Friends

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Lucy P. Morton of Westport to "Dear Friends". Congratulations "friends" on becoming parents. Railroad from New York to Boston runs through town. Has been completed but a short time. Likes place where she lives. Husband has been on lecturing tour. Speaks of "California rage".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scriptural Reference - "He is stiled by his subject, the shadow of God a God on Earth Brother to the Sun and Moon. Disposes of all Earthly Crowns".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement that Publick Universal Friend had paid $579.28 at sundry times on Chancery business.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of land conveyances to Rachel Malin. Total of 4,160 acres: to Jacob Wagener 1803 acres; to David Wagener 711 acres; Mary Aldrig, 335 acres. Conveyances by William Carter by order of Thomas Hathaway.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theme is power of God over mortals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Half a letter signed by Lucy Brown with reference to lands.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of division of 1,100 acres of land between William Potter, B. Brown, E. Sharman, etc., signed by James Parker.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written in memory of James Bird of Kingston Volunteers who marched to Erie under command of Captain Thomas to protect western frontiers evidently during war of 1812.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A list of James Parker's Associates delivered to Benjamin Banta by him for a guide in their contract. March 15

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermon by Jemima Wilkinson. A Short dialogue Between A Larned Divine and a beggar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter - Exeter, Rhode Island, from Ruth Richard to Friends, in the nature of a sermon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, Philadelphia, from Christopher Marshall to the Universal Friend (and Sarah Richard) in answer to a letter written by the friend. Religious

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of confession by Alice Hazzard, that she took a considerable sum of money from her father-in-law, an act of which the Friend was accused. Endorsement states copy dated, June 10, 1790.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copy of letter from Mehitabel Smith, Groton to Abner Brownell, asking him not to advertise a misunderstanding between them.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, Hudson City, James Parker to Abraham Dayton at New Milford. Parker describes himself as an agent for the Friends in matter of purchase of Boston preemption and claims he has saved them $100,000.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Promise, South Kingston by Benedict Robinson to Alice Hazzard to deliver to her or by her order a silver tea-pot and other items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A Journal of Ruth Spencer, describing briefly the Friend's early activities and giving the texts of her sermons.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Last Will and Testament of William Turpin, formerly of Charleston, S. C., late of New York, who died, January 21, 1835, in the 81st year of his age. New York, 1835

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, New York, William Turpin to Rachel Malin of Jerusalem, Yates County, describing a trip to Philadelphia and Washington and a conversation with President Jackson.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement of Peleg Brown of cash collected on account of Rachel Malin of Jerusalem, Yates County, with letter from Brown asking Rachel to have her stocks transferred to her own name.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agreement among certain purchasers of land in a tract granted to James Parker, William Potter, and Thomas Hathaway and their associates (Society of Friends) to share the costs of defending suits for land claims brought against the Society of Friends.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter, Watervliet, William Carter to Rachel Malin concerning suit Carter threatens to bring against Rachel Malin unless she takes his advice and turns over land she claims to him.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Minutes of a meeting and resolutions, the community of Friends, Jerusalem, concerning a contract to buy land west of Seneca Lake and an agreement with James Parker to fulfill the contract.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Indenture, Thomas Hathaway and Benedict Robinson of Jerusalem, by Power of Attorney from William Carter of Albany, to Jacob Wagener, for land in Township 7, Range 2 for 100 York currency.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notice, Geneva, of auction of land in gore for default on payment of mortgage to Abel Densmore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Description of mansion five miles west of Penn Yan erected by Arnold Potter in the late 18th century and of visit by Louis Philippe.
